# Habitat Website: Light Mode + New Pages

## Context
The Habitat homepage (`/Users/canerden/Downloads/habitat-homepage.html`) was already revised by Claude Opus to reflect the full ecosystem (evenings + hackathons + residency). The user now wants to:
1. Convert it from dark to **light mode**
2. Add **3 new pages**: Residency, Hackathon, Contact/Apply
3. Wire the contact form to **Google Sheets** via Google Apps Script
4. Keep everything as zero-dependency static HTML files (no frameworks)

The end goal is a self-contained folder deployable to Antigravity or Netlify.

---

## Deliverables

### File structure
```
habitat-website/
├── index.html          ← revised homepage (light mode)
├── residency.html      ← new
├── hackathon.html      ← new
├── contact.html        ← new (with Google Sheets form)
└── images/             ← placeholder folder (user adds photos)
    ├── event-1.jpg … event-5.jpg
    ├── logos/
    └── people/
```

---

## Step 1 — Convert homepage to light mode

Change CSS variables in `index.html`:

| Variable | Old (dark) | New (light) |
|---|---|---|
| `--black` | `#0a0a0a` | `#fafaf8` (warm white bg) |
| `--white` | `#f5f2eb` | `#1a1918` (near-black text) |
| `--cream` | `#ede9df` | `#f0ede6` (light section bg) |
| `--gray` | `#8a8780` | `#6b6864` (secondary text) |
| `--gray-light` | `#c4c1b8` | `#9e9b96` (muted) |
| `--accent` | `#3d5afc` | `#3d5afc` (keep) |
| Teal/Purple/Coral | keep values | adjust if too faint on light |

Additional light-mode CSS changes:
- Nav background: `rgba(250, 250, 248, 0.95)` with `backdrop-filter: blur(20px)`
- Card backgrounds: `#ffffff` or `#f5f2eb` with subtle border `1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.08)`
- Stats bar: light background variant
- Photo strip background: `#f0ede6`
- Footer: `#1a1918` (dark footer still works, gives contrast)
- Box shadows: `rgba(0,0,0,0.06)` instead of dark glows

---

## Step 2 — Create `residency.html`

Sections:
1. **Nav** (shared design, links to all pages)
2. **Hero** — "Live and build with 4 other founders for 7 days" + "Coming 2026" badge
3. **What is the Residency** — format overview (dates, location, cohort size = 5)
4. **Program structure** — daily schedule (morning work, afternoon mentorship, evening demos)
5. **Mentorship** — dedicated mentor, VC access, peer network
6. **Demo Day** — culminating event, pitch to sponsors and network
7. **Who it's for** — pre-revenue or early-revenue technical founders
8. **Apply / Join waitlist** — CTA linking to contact.html with `?program=residency`

---

## Step 3 — Create `hackathon.html`

Sections:
1. **Nav**
2. **Hero** — "One day. One team. One working product." + format callout
3. **Format** — full day (10am–8pm), teams of 2–3, technical output = working demo
4. **Who attends** — developers, technical founders, CS students
5. **Sponsors** — VC and YC founder backing, what they offer (prizes, cloud credits, deal flow)
6. **How it ends** — pitches directly to sponsors, feedback, connections
7. **Past events / stats** (if available, otherwise Coming Soon)
8. **Register / Interest form** — CTA to contact.html with `?program=hackathon`

---

## Step 4 — Create `contact.html` (Google Sheets form)

### Form fields
- Name
- Email
- Which program: dropdown (Evenings / Hackathon / Residency / General question)
- Message / anything you want us to know
- Submit button

### Google Sheets connection (Google Apps Script)

**User will do once (setup steps included as comments in the file):**
1. Create a new Google Sheet
2. Open Extensions → Apps Script, paste the Web App script
3. Deploy as Web App (execute as: Me, access: Anyone)
4. Copy the deployment URL → paste into `contact.html`

**Apps Script (included in contact.html as a comment block):**
```javascript
function doPost(e) {
  var sheet = SpreadsheetApp.getActiveSpreadsheet().getActiveSheet();
  var data = JSON.parse(e.postData.contents);
  sheet.appendRow([new Date(), data.name, data.email, data.program, data.message]);
  return ContentService
    .createTextOutput(JSON.stringify({ status: 'ok' }))
    .setMimeType(ContentService.MimeType.JSON);
}
```

**Form JS (in contact.html):**
```javascript
form.addEventListener('submit', async (e) => {
  e.preventDefault();
  const payload = { name, email, program, message };
  await fetch('YOUR_APPS_SCRIPT_URL', {
    method: 'POST',
    body: JSON.stringify(payload)
  });
  // Show success message
});
```

No CORS issues — Google Apps Script handles it natively.

---

## Step 5 — Update navigation on all pages

Each page gets the same nav with links:
- **[Habitat]** logo → index.html
- Evenings → index.html#products
- Hackathons → hackathon.html
- Residency → residency.html
- Apply → contact.html

---

## Verification

1. Open each HTML file directly in a browser (no server needed for static files)
2. Check light mode renders correctly across all sections
3. Test contact form: fill it out, check Google Sheet receives the row
4. Test nav links work across all 4 pages
5. Test `?program=hackathon` pre-selects the right dropdown option
6. Resize to mobile (768px) to check responsive layout

---

## Critical file
- `/Users/canerden/Downloads/habitat-homepage.html` — source of truth for design system

[Habitat]
  • Hackathons
  • Residency
  • Why Habitat
  • Apply →
Technical Hackathons

One day. One team.
One working product.

Full-day deep builds for developers and technical founders. Investor-backed. Real infrastructure. Real stakes. Ship or learn — either way you win.

Duration 10am – 8pm
Team size 2-3 people
Backed by VC + YC founders
Output Working demo
Register interest → See the format
The format

10 hours of focused building.

No slides. No pitch decks at the start. You arrive, you form a team, you build. At the end, you demo a working product to the investors in the room.

10:00

Kickoff & theme reveal

The technical theme or problem space is announced. Team up, pick your angle, define your scope. You have 30 minutes to align — then you build.

10:30

Building begins

Heads down. Real code, real infrastructure, real product. Mentors from our sponsor network are in the room to unblock you when you're stuck.

13:00

Lunch + mid-day check-in

Break, eat, recalibrate. Optional 5-minute mid-day check-ins with a mentor if you've hit a wall or want to pressure-test your approach.

14:00

Back to building

Afternoon block. Focus on making it work end-to-end, not just look good. A working demo matters more than a polished UI.

18:00

Freeze + prep demos

Code freeze. Two hours to prep your demo — no new features, just make it presentable and tell the story clearly.

19:00

Demo + investor pitches

Each team gets 5 minutes. Live demo, one slide max. Sponsors ask real questions. The best teams get follow-up conversations, not just a prize.

20:00

Awards + networking

Prizes, credits, and introductions. More importantly: the people in this room are who you want to know.

Who attends

Built for people who ship.

This isn't a general hackathon. We select for technical ability and the drive to build real things — not to win competitions.

⚡

Technical founders

You've already shipped something. You build fast. You want a focused day with peers at your level and direct access to investors who get technical products.

🛠

Developers exploring startups

You're a strong engineer who's been thinking about building your own thing. This is a low-stakes way to test if you like the founder experience.

🎓

CS & engineering students

From KU Leuven, UGent, VUB, or elsewhere. You want to build something real, meet people who are doing it, and get in front of investors early.

Backing this

Real investors.
Real deal flow.

Our technical hackathons are backed by investors who want to see what you build — and follow up with the best teams.

Lead sponsor

VC Partner

An active early-stage VC fund backing our first technical hackathons. They're there to find deal flow, mentor builders, and write checks to the right teams.

  • Attend and judge the demo day
  • First look at top teams
  • Cloud credits & API access for participants
Co-sponsor

YC Founder

A YC founder co-sponsoring the event to give back to the technical community — and to find the next generation of builders they want to advise.

  • Office hours during the event
  • Direct intros to their network
  • Prizes for winning teams
What you walk away with

More than a weekend project.

The goal isn't to win a hackathon. The goal is to leave with something worth continuing — and people worth building with.

A working technical demo

Not a mockup. Not a pitch deck. A live product that does something real — and that you built under pressure in 8 hours.

Investor introductions

The sponsors are in the room all day. If your build gets their attention, the conversation starts that evening — not 6 months later.

Your co-founder, maybe

Teams form on the day. Some of the best founder partnerships start at events like this. You won't know until you build together.

Ready to build?

Register your interest and we'll notify you when the next technical hackathon opens.

Register interest → Back to Habitat

Programs

Evenings Technical Hackathons Residency

Community

Events calendar LinkedIn Instagram

Contact

[Habitat]
  • Hackathons
  • Residency
  • Why Habitat
  • Apply →
Coming 2026
Habitat Residency

Live and build with
4 other founders
for 7 days.

You've already shipped an MVP. Now it's time to go deeper. Full immersion, dedicated mentorship, and a demo day in front of investors.

Duration 7 days
Cohort size 5 founders
Format Live-in
Ends with Demo day
Join the waitlist → How it works
What it is

From MVP to
business in a week.

Habitat Residency is not a course. Not a coworking space. Not another accelerator. It's one week where you only build.

The premise is simple.

Five founders, already past idea stage, live and work together for seven consecutive days. No commute. No context-switching. No meetings except the ones that matter.

You get a dedicated mentor — a practitioner, not a professor — who works alongside you for the full week. You get access to a VC network for feedback and potential funding. And on day seven, you pitch.

The goal isn't to raise money that week (though it happens). The goal is to leave knowing exactly where you're going and why.

5
founders per cohort — small enough to build real trust
7
consecutive days of focused, undistracted building
1
dedicated mentor with you the entire week
0
equity taken — ever
The week

Seven days, structured to ship.

Each day has a clear theme. You're building the whole time — but we make sure you're building the right things in the right order.

Day 1

Arrivals + alignment

Move in, meet your cohort, set your week goal. What does success look like by Friday? Get brutally honest about where you are.

Day 2

Customer conversations

Your first full day is not building. It's talking to users. Structured interviews, real insights, kill your assumptions early.

Day 3 — 4

Deep build

Two full days of heads-down work. Morning standups with your mentor. Afternoon unblocking sessions. Evening reviews with the cohort.

Day 5

Test & iterate

Get real users on your product. Run experiments. Fix what's broken. Stop adding features. Make what you have work better.

Day 6

Story + pitch prep

What's your narrative? Why this, why now, why you? Practice your demo until the story is effortless.

Day 7

Demo Day

Investors, mentors, and the broader Habitat community. 10-minute demo + Q&A. The beginning of the next chapter.

Support structure

Not advice. Presence.

The difference between a good program and a transformative one is whether the people helping you are actually in the room.

Dedicated mentor

One practitioner — a founder, operator, or investor who has done what you're trying to do — works with your cohort the full week. Not for 2 hours. For the week.

VC office hours

Investors from our network drop in for sessions throughout the week. Not to evaluate you — to help you think clearer. Evaluation happens at Demo Day, not before.

Peer cohort

Four other founders at the same stage, with complementary skills, working alongside you. The cohort is small enough to build genuine trust — and honest enough to tell you hard truths.

Who we select

Already past zero.

The Residency is for founders who've proven they can ship — and are ready to figure out whether they can build a business.

✓

You've shipped an MVP

You have a live product — not a landing page, not a deck. Real code, real users, even if early. You know you can build.

✓

You're pre or early revenue

You have some validation but you're not yet at scale. The Residency accelerates the period between "it works" and "it grows."

✓

You can be all-in for a week

No day job meetings. No half-presence. Seven days of full focus. If you can't commit to that, the timing isn't right yet.

✓

You want to be challenged

The cohort and mentor will push you. Good feedback is uncomfortable. You need to want that, not just tolerate it.

Not for everyone

Be honest with yourself.

The Residency will make great founders better. It won't turn someone who isn't ready into a founder.

✕
You're still validating whether you want to be a founder
✕
You haven't built or shipped anything yet
✕
You're looking for co-founders (try our evenings first)
✕
You can't step away from your current obligations for a week
The culmination

Demo Day.

Day seven isn't a formality. It's the moment your week of work meets the people who can help you go further.

Demo Day is a curated event open to investors from our network, the broader Habitat community, and select guests. Each founder has 10 minutes: a live product demo, not slides.

Q&A follows. The conversation continues at the networking session after. Some founders leave with term sheets. Most leave with introductions, feedback, and a clearer path.

All of them leave knowing exactly what the next 90 days look like.

Live product demos only

No decks. No mockups. Your product, live, in front of people who matter.

Curated investor audience

Early-stage VCs and angels from our network — people who write checks at this stage.

Habitat community

Alumni from our evenings and hackathons. Your first potential early adopters are in the room.

Post-event introductions

We personally make intros from Demo Day onwards. The event is a start, not a finish.

Applications open in 2026.

Join the waitlist and we'll reach out when the first cohort opens. Cohort size is 5. We're selective.

Join the waitlist → Back to Habitat

Community

Events calendar LinkedIn Instagram

Contact

I'm working through alternative framings for the three core value propositions. Instead of listing what Habitat isn't, I should emphasize what makes it unique — the merit-based progression where builders get invited up based on what they ship, the ongoing community aspect rather than a one-time cohort experience, and the fact that there's no equity stake or application barrier. The simplest fix might be to just reframe the headline to "Not an accelerator. Good catch. "Not a hackathon" directly contradicts the hackathon page. The whole section needs a rethink around the full ecosystem. Here's my suggested rewrite:

---

**Current headline:** "Not an accelerator. Not a hackathon."
→ contradicts your own product

**New direction:** The differentiator isn't the format — it's the model. No equity, community-selected (you prove yourself by building, not applying), and a continuous ecosystem vs. a one-off event.

Here's a rewrite for the full section:

---

**Label:** Why Habitat

**Headline:** "Built different. No equity. No noise."

**Sub:** "Most programs pick you. We watch you build. Start with an evening. Go deeper when you're ready."

**3 points:**

**Test before you commit**
Most startup advice skips the hard part: you don't know if an idea is worth six months until you've shown it to real people. Habitat lets you validate in an evening before deciding whether to go deeper.

**You rise by building, not applying**
We don't select founders from applications. The best people in our evenings get invited to hackathons. The best hackathon teams get first look at Residency spots. Merit shows up in code, not cover letters.

**No equity. Ever.**
Accelerators take 7%. We take nothing. Our sponsors fund these events because they want deal flow — which means our incentives are perfectly aligned with yours.

---

Everything's on GitHub. Now to deploy on Vercel:

1. Go to [vercel.com](https://vercel.com) → **Add New Project**
2. Import `ycanerden/habitat-website` from GitHub
3. Framework preset: **Other** (it's plain HTML, no framework)
4. Leave all defaults → click **Deploy**

Vercel will give you a `.vercel.app` URL instantly. To connect your `joinhabitat.eu` domain, go to the project settings → Domains → add it there.

And yes — you can make the GitHub repo private at any time from the repo Settings → Danger Zone → Change visibility. Vercel will still be able to deploy from it.
